summary: Ukrainian air defenses downed 115 of 124 Shahed‑type UAVs during a massive night raid, while Russian forces continued guided‑bomb strikes on Donetsk and eastern Dnipropetrovsk. UAV activity intensified across the north‑east, with multiple Shahed drones moving toward Chernihiv, Poltava and the Black Sea. Ground attacks persisted, highlighted by a Russian FPV‑drone strike on Marhanets and Ukrainian UAV raids on Novorossisk.
key_points: Ukrainian air defenses shot down 115 of 124 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones launched from Kursk, Shatalovo, Orel, Bryansk, Millerovo, Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, Gvardeyskoye and Crimea; 7 strike hits recorded on five locations (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical aviation released guided aerial bombs on Donetsk and east Dnipropetrovsk, and on eastern Zaporizhzhia toward southern Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @kpszsu/62443).
Multiple Shahed UAVs reported over Sumy moving west toward Chernihiv, over Poltava heading to the city, and over the Black Sea toward Tatarbunary (Source: @kpszsu/62453).
A Russian FPV drone struck near a service vehicle in Marhanets, injuring 13 civilians with moderate wounds (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted a strike on Novorossisk, indicating expanded southern‑front air operations (Source: @eRadarrua/93006).
A Russian Orlan‑10 reconnaissance UAV operated over the Black Sea opposite Zatoka, monitoring maritime traffic (Source: @eRadarrua/92986).
A reactive missile was observed near Ponornytsia, heading toward Koryukivka, suggesting artillery‑rocket activity on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92997).
Ukrainian forces continued building engineer‑fortified positions in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions to strengthen static defence (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses downed 115 of 124 Shahed‑type UAVs during a massive night raid, while Russian forces continued guided‑bomb strikes on Donetsk and eastern Dnipropetrovsk. UAV activity intensified across the north‑east, with multiple Shahed drones moving toward Chernihiv, Poltava and the Black Sea. Ground attacks persisted, highlighted by a Russian FPV‑drone strike on Marhanets and Ukrainian UAV raids on Novorossisk.

points: Ukrainian air defence units, including aviation, SAM brigades, REB and mobile fire groups, intercepted 115 hostile UAVs (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, imitation types) across the north, south and east, preventing further damage (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian engineers are constructing fortified positions in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk, building on lessons from Avdiivka and earlier battles to improve static defence (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).
Ukrainian UAVs successfully struck Novorossisk, extending offensive air operations into the southern front and targeting Russian logistics (Source: @eRadarrua/93006).
Ukrainian forces reported a reactive missile launch near Ponornytsia, heading toward Koryukivka, indicating active counter‑rocket measures on the northern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/92997).

Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Donetsk and eastern Dnipropetrovsk, as well as on eastern Zaporizhzhia toward southern Dnipropetrovsk, aiming to disrupt Ukrainian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62443).
A Russian FPV drone attack near Marhanets damaged a service vehicle and injured 13 civilians, showing continued use of low‑cost loitering munitions against infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).

Russian Shahed‑type UAVs launched from multiple western Russian regions (Kursk, Shatalovo, Orel, Bryansk, Millerovo, Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, Gvardeyskoye, Crimea) targeted Ukrainian territory, reflecting a broad launch envelope (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
A Russian Orlan‑10 reconnaissance UAV operated over the Black Sea opposite Zatoka, likely monitoring Ukrainian naval movements (Source: @eRadarrua/92986).
UAVs from Sumy moved westward toward Chernihiv, increasing pressure on the northern‑eastern axis and threatening logistics corridors (Source: @kpszsu/62453).
Shahed drones were observed heading eastward over Dnipropetrovsk toward Pavlograd, indicating a focus on the eastern industrial belt (Source: @kpszsu/62431).
Russian guided‑bomb strikes on Donetsk and east Dnipropetrovsk aim to degrade Ukrainian command and supply nodes in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/62443).
Ukrainian fortified engineering works in Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk enhance defensive depth against further Russian offensives (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).
Shahed UAVs launched from Russian western regions penetrated northern Ukraine, prompting extensive air‑defence engagements (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
UAV activity over Shostka prompted civilian shelter alerts, reflecting ongoing aerial threat in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62419).
Reactive missile near Ponornytsia heading to Koryukivka shows active artillery exchanges on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92997).
UAVs from Sumy moving toward Chernihiv increase pressure on the northern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/62453).
Ukrainian UAV strike on Novorossisk demonstrates expanded southern‑front air operations against Russian‑held territories (Source: @eRadarrua/93006).
Russian FPV‑drone attack on Marhanets caused civilian casualties, highlighting persistent low‑altitude threats in the southern industrial zone (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).
Guided‑bomb releases toward eastern Zaporizhzhia and southern Dnipropetrovsk target key logistics hubs in the south (Source: @kpszsu/62439).
Orlan‑10 UAV over the Black Sea near Zatoka monitors maritime traffic, potentially supporting southern‑front naval operations (Source: @eRadarrua/92986).
Ukrainian air defence shot down 115 of 124 hostile UAVs during a coordinated night raid, with engagements across north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian Shahed, Gerbera and Italmas UAVs launched from at least eight Russian locations, indicating a wide‑area launch strategy (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian UAVs operating over Sumy, Poltava and the Black Sea show expanding Ukrainian aerial reconnaissance and strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/62453).
Russian Orlan‑10 reconnaissance UAV activity over the Black Sea suggests continued aerial ISR on maritime approaches (Source: @eRadarrua/92986).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defence downed 115 of 124 Shahed‑type UAVs launched from Kursk, Shatalovo, Orel, Bryansk, Millerovo, Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, Gvardeyskoye and Crimea, with seven strike impacts recorded on five locations (Source: @kpszsu/62415).

Russian tactical aviation released guided aerial bombs on Donetsk and east Dnipropetrovsk, and on eastern Zaporizhzhia toward southern Dnipropetrovsk, targeting command and logistics nodes (Source: @kpszsu/62443).

Multiple Shahed UAVs were reported moving from Sumy toward Chernihiv, from Poltava toward the city, and over the Black Sea toward Tatarbunary, indicating a broadening of the UAV threat corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62453).

A Russian FPV drone struck near a service vehicle in Marhanets, injuring 13 civilians with moderate wounds, highlighting the continued use of loitering munitions against civilian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81386).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted a strike on Novorossisk, extending offensive air operations into the southern front and targeting Russian supply lines (Source: @eRadarrua/93006).
A Russian Orlan‑10 reconnaissance UAV operated over the Black Sea opposite Zatoka, likely monitoring Ukrainian naval movements (Source: @eRadarrua/92986).
A reactive missile was observed near Ponornytsia, heading toward Koryukivka, suggesting active artillery‑rocket engagements on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/92997).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are employing a diversified UAV and guided‑bomb campaign, launching from multiple western Russian bases to pressure Ukrainian airspace across all fronts. Ukrainian air defences have shown resilience, intercepting the majority of incoming drones, yet the sheer volume indicates a sustained Russian aerial threat. Concurrently, low‑cost loitering munitions and reconnaissance UAVs are being used to target civilian infrastructure and gather maritime intelligence, complicating Ukraine's defensive posture.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing northern and eastern sectors with fortified engineering works and increased air‑defence deployments, while expanding UAV strike capabilities into the southern theater. International partners continue to supply air‑defence systems, enabling the high interception rate observed. Russian reliance on a broad launch footprint suggests attempts to overwhelm Ukrainian defenses, prompting Kyiv to prioritize layered air‑defence integration across all axes.
sections.intelligence_notes: The concentration of UAV launch sites in western Russia points to a logistical hub network that can be targeted for disruption. The emergence of Ukrainian UAV strikes on Novorossisk indicates a shift toward deeper penetration missions, requiring enhanced Russian air‑defence coverage in occupied territories. Continued FPV‑drone attacks on civilian sites underscore the need for improved low‑altitude detection and rapid response capabilities.
